channel.title: Robert Akhmerov | v31dt
channel.description: Recent content on Robert Akhmerov | v31dt

item.title: About Me
item.description:
I am Robert Akhmerov, also known online as v31dt. I study Applied Computer Science at Howest, majoring in Cybersecurity, and I am currently building toward a junior penetration tester or security engineering role.

My work focuses on practical security: web and mobile penetration testing, Burp Suite workflows, Android security assessment, malware analysis, reverse engineering, and automation. During my offensive security internship at KBC, I worked on mobile penetration testing automation, Burp Suite and Frida integration, OWASP MASVS-based assessment, and Android banking malware research.

item.title: How I Built This Portfolio
item.description:
This portfolio is built with Hugo, a static site generator. I chose Hugo because it keeps the portfolio simple, fast, and easy to maintain. Each article is written as a Markdown file, and Hugo converts those files into a static website.

The visual theme is Archie, a minimal Hugo theme. I selected it because it keeps the focus on the content: reflections, projects, and proof of professional development.

The source code is hosted in the GitHub repository v31dt/v31dt.github.io. GitHub Pages publishes the repository from the main branch. The generated Hugo output is committed in the repository root so the current GitHub Pages configuration can serve the website directly.

item.title: KBC Offensive Security Internship
item.description:
Context

From February 2026 to May 2026, I completed an offensive security internship at KBC. The work focused on mobile penetration testing, Android banking malware research, and automation for security testing environments.

Technologies

  • Burp Suite
  • Frida
  • OWASP MASVS
  • Android security testing
  • Malware analysis tooling
  • Automation scripting

My Contribution

I developed automation for mobile penetration testing environments, integrated Burp Suite with Frida for login flow automation, contributed to a KBC mobile security assessment based on OWASP MASVS, and researched Android banking malware.

item.title: Future of Bug Bounty and How to Build Your Own HackBot
item.description:
For my Professional Networking podcast assignment, I spoke with Pedro Paniago, Offensive Security Manager at PwC Belgium, bug hunter, and a distinct voice in Belgium’s security scene.

Topic

The episode focuses on the future of bug bounty, AI’s influence on bug hunting, and how security researchers can build their own AI hackbot from scratch.

Guest

Pedro Paniago is an Offensive Security Manager at PwC Belgium. Besides his professional work, he is active in bug hunting and regularly shares a practical view on offensive security, automation, and the changing role of AI in cybersecurity.

item.title: CSCBE 2026 ECSC Track Finalist
item.description:
Context

In February 2026, I ranked 8th nationally in the Cyber Security Challenge Belgium Junior track qualifiers and was selected for the ECSC track finals.

Reflection

The competition gave me a useful benchmark outside school. It tested speed, problem solving, and technical range, while also connecting me more directly with the Belgian cybersecurity community.

item.title: CVE-2026-25471
item.description:
Context

In February 2026, I discovered and responsibly disclosed CVE-2026-25471, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in a WordPress “Admin Safety” plugin.

Impact

The vulnerability could enable account takeover. The work required vulnerability research, validation, impact analysis, and responsible disclosure.

Reflection

This was security research outside normal coursework. It taught me how important validation and responsible disclosure are, especially when a vulnerability has real account takeover impact.

item.title: AInalyzer
item.description:
Context

AInalyzer is an open-source AI-powered Burp Suite extension. It integrates an AI assistant into HTTP endpoint security investigations while keeping a human in the loop.

Technologies

  • Burp Suite extension development
  • AI-assisted analysis
  • HTTP security testing
  • Human-in-the-loop workflows

Reflection

AInalyzer reflects my interest in combining AI with practical security work. The main idea is not to replace the tester, but to make investigation faster while keeping human judgement in control.

item.title: ELK + Burp Suite Monitoring System
item.description:
Context

This project built a Burp Suite and ELK Stack setup for centralized storage, analysis, and anomaly detection of HTTP requests.

Technologies

  • Burp Suite
  • Elasticsearch
  • Logstash / Beats
  • Kibana
  • Filebeat
  • HTTP request analysis

Result

The setup supports security research and vulnerability tracking by collecting and analyzing HTTP traffic in a centralized monitoring stack.

Reflection

This project connected penetration testing workflows with monitoring and analysis. It pushed me to think beyond individual findings and look at how request data can be stored, searched, and used during longer security investigations.
